{"number":57467,"title":"[BUG] Handle user concent using the Azure.AI.Projects SDK","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2

### Describe the bug

I have an application calling the AI Agent in the new Foundry using Azure.AI.Projects. The agent has one tool registered using `OAuth Identity Passthrough` Authentication.

As long as the agent is not using any tools, it works, but when it does, I get an empty response.

If I make the Agent call using my own DefaultAzureCredential, it works if I have tested the agent from the portal and given concent, while if I use a service principle always fails. 

I use the `CreateResponseStreamingAsync`:
```csharp
currentResponseId = await StreamRoundAsync(
    responseClient.CreateResponseStreamingAsync(followUpOptions, cancellationToken),
    onToken, onToolCall, cancellationToken,
    pendingApprovals);

...

private async Task StreamRoundAsync(
    IAsyncEnumerable stream,
    Func onToken,
    Func? onToolCall,
    CancellationToken cancellationToken,
    List pendingApprovals)
{
    string? responseId = null;
    await foreach (var update in stream.WithCancellation(cancellationToken))
    {
        _logger.LogInformation("Agent update: {Type}", update.GetType().Name);

        if (update is StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate createdUpdate)
        {
            responseId = createdUpdate.Response.Id;
        }
        else if (update is StreamingResponseOutputTextDeltaUpdate textDelta &&
                    !string.IsNullOrEmpty(textDelta.Delta))
        {
            await onToken(textDelta.Delta);
        }
        else if (update is StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate itemDone &&
                    itemDone.Item is McpToolCallApprovalRequestItem approvalRequest)
        {
            var requestItem = itemDone.Item as McpToolCallApprovalRequestItem;
            var toolName = requestItem?.ToolName ?? "UnknownTool";
            _logger.LogWarning("MCP approval request on server: {Server} {ToolName}", approvalRequest.ServerLabel, toolName);

            if (onToolCall != null)
                await onToolCall(approvalRequest.ServerLabel ?? string.Empty);
            pendingApprovals.Add(approvalRequest);
        }
                
    }
    return responseId;
}
```

## Scenario 1: Using my own credentials for the first time or using as service priciple
The agent returns an empty response
StreamingResponseUpdate events:
```bash
      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsInProgressUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsFailedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate
      Agent update: UnknownResponseStreamEvent
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseCompletedUpdate
```
## Scenario 2: Using my own credentials AFTER concent in portal
The agent returns an empty response
StreamingResponseUpdate events:
```bash
      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsInProgressUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsCompletedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate
      MCP approval request on server: IntuneMcpTool list_devices
      Agent update: StreamingResponseCompletedUpdate
      Auto-approving MCP tool call on: IntuneMcpTool
      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallInProgressUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallArgumentsDeltaUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallArgumentsDoneUpdate
      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallCompletedUpdate
```


### Expected behavior

I can handle approval of using tools, but not **user concent**. I would like there to be an event where I can hamdle user concent.

PS. The fact that it "works" using my own account proviced I've given concent in the portal is strange to me., and only works until the agent hibernates.


### Actual behavior

No event is raised allowing me to accept and give concent

### Reproduction Steps

See code above

* Cognitive Services OpenAI User

* Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1

Azure.AI open telemetry logging is inconsistent between the python and .net SDKs","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Ai.Projects 1.0.0-beta11

### Describe the bug

When following the same exact steps for configuring/enabling open telemetry as described here:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/develop/trace-application
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/how-to/develop/trace-agents-sdk

I am unable to get it so that the Azure Foundry Traces Dashboard/Tab shows anything from our .net SDK usage.

I dug into the app insights query the Traces tab uses, and found where the root cause of the issue.

In short, the telemetry just has a fundamentally different structure.  The calls that the dashboard attempts to identify as the "root" records are those that either lack a `parent_operationId ` value, or where the `parent_operationId ` equals the `operation_id`.

In the .net SDK - no matter what I do - even if I disable all telemetry outside of what the Azure SDK is doing - there is never a time where these records are pointed to themselves as a parent (or lack a parent altogether).

This is the first half of the query the Foundry dashboard is running:

```
// Helper function to determine Gen AI role based on event name
let get_role = (event_name: string) { 
    iff(event_name == "gen_ai.choice", "assistant", split(event_name, ".")[1])
};
let get_event_name = (customDimensions: dynamic, message: string) {  iff(customDimensions["event.name"] == "", message, customDimensions["event.name"]) };
let get_response_id = (customDimensions: dynamic) { 
    iff(
        customDimensions["gen_ai.response.id"] == "", 
        iff(customDimensions["gen_ai.thread.run.id"] == "", "", strcat(tostring(customDimensions["gen_ai.thread.id"]), "/", tostring(customDimensions["gen_ai.thread.run.id"]))),
        tostring(customDimensions["gen_ai.response.id"]))
}; 
let is_completion_message = (customDimensions: dynamic, event_name: string) { event_name == "gen_ai.choice" or (event_name == "gen_ai.assistant.message" and customDimensions["gen_ai.thread.run.id"] != "") };
let get_evaluator_name = (customDimensions: dynamic, event_name: string) { iff(customDimensions["gen_ai.evaluator.name"] == "", split(event_name, ".")[2], tostring(customDimensions["gen_ai.evaluator.name"])) };
// Retrieve all GenAI operations
let gen_ai_operations = requests | union dependencies
| where isnotnull(customDimensions["gen_ai.system"])
| summarize count() by operation_Id;
let gen_ai_prompts_and_completions = traces
    | extend event_name = get_event_name(customDimensions, message)
    | where (event_name startswith "gen_ai") and (event_name !startswith "gen_ai.evaluation")
    | extend event_content = iff(message startswith ("gen_ai"), tostring(customDimensions["gen_ai.event.content"]), message)
    | extend json = parse_json(event_content)
    | extend role = get_role(event_name)
    | extend content = bag_merge(bag_pack("timestamp", timestamp, "role", role), iff(json["message"] != "", json.message, json))
    | extend is_completion = is_completion_message(customDimensions, event_name)    
    | project 
        trace_operation_id = operation_Id,
        trace_parent_id = operation_ParentId, 
        trace_response_id = get_response_id(customDimensions),       
        trace_prompt = iff(is_completion, dynamic(null), content),
        trace_completion = iff(is_completion, content, dynamic(null)),
        trace_content = content,
        trace_role = role,
        trace_timestamp = timestamp;
// Retrieve root span for each operation
gen_ai_operations | join kind=inner (union requests, dependencies) on operation_Id
  | where operation_ParentId == "" or operation_Id == operation_ParentId
  | project root_name = name, root_start_time = timestamp, root_span_type = type, root_duration = duration, root_success = success, root_span_id = id, operation_Id;

- MCPToolDefinition does not support the project_connection_id property like in Python MCPTool class","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

The class MCPToolDefinition has no way to set the `project_connection_id` property like we can on the [MCPTool class](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-ai-agents/azure.ai.agents.models.mcptool?view=azure-python-preview) in python.

The `project_connection_id` is a property that is necessary when creating an agent that uses a MCP tool already created in Foundry nextgen.

Here are some examples in python:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/main/sdk/ai/azure-ai-projects/samples/agents/tools/sample_agent_mcp_with_project_connection.py#L48

```python
    tool = MCPTool(
        server_label="api-specs",
        server_url="https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp",
        require_approval="always",
        project_connection_id=os.environ["MCP_PROJECT_CONNECTION_ID"],
    )
```

I believe the `project_connection_id` is metadata, in that it does not need to be in the MCPToolResource (i.e. per thread run) thus why it could be in the MCPToolDefinition.

### Expected behavior

Be able to have extra dynamic parameters that can be passed to the REST API of Foundry to align with the Foundry nextgen platform.

### Actual behavior

Impossible to set the the property in any shape or form.

### Reproduction Steps

```csharp
new MCPToolDefinition("MCP-Server-Label", "MCP-Remote-URL")
```

MCPToolDefinition does not expose the `project_connection_id` property

### Describe the bug

When I use the SDK to get CustomKeys connections, the Credentials part is not serialized correctly. The actual response is compatible with the `DeserializeCustomCredential` method.

There is no "keys" properties in the response inside the "credentials" object.

The raw response from the api looks like:
```json
{
  "name" : "**omit**",
  "id" : "**omit**",
  "type" : "CustomKeys",
  "target" : "_",
  "isDefault" : false,
  "credentials" : {

  },

    "baseUrl" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**",
    "username" : "**CORRECT_VALUE**"
  }

The `DeserializeCustomCredential` tries to read the key `credentials.keys` as a nested object to create the `Keys` property of the `CustomCredential.Credentials.Keys`

```json
{

  "credentials": {
    "type": "CustomKeys",
    "keys": {

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** You're reporting that when using `Azure.AI.Projects` with an AI Foundry agent that has OAuth Identity Passthrough authentication configured for a tool, there is no SDK-level event to handle user consent. When consent hasn't been granted, the tool call silently fails (with `StreamingResponseMcpListToolsFailedUpdate`) and the agent returns an empty response with no mechanism for the application to prompt or handle user consent programmatically.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2`  
**Affected API:** `CreateResponseStreamingAsync` / streaming response events  
**Scenarios:**
